situational_context: Running comeback Week 5: 7 runs completed since gap broken May 3 (after 182-day layoff). Latest Run #7 (Jun 3): 5.23 km, FC 140, max 161, suffer 27 — 2nd successful 5 km validation (Run #5 was first on May 23). VO2max running improved to 39.9 (from 38.3 at restart). Primary modality remains e-bike commuting on 'sur site' days (~38 km RT). Chronic load declining to 15.6 (from 22.3 on May 14) due to fewer commute days and training gaps — needs stabilization. ACWR 0.88 — in safe rebuild zone. Weight continuing to decline: 103.0 kg (down from 104.1 at May 14). HRV stable at 36 (BALANCED). Training readiness 57 (moderate, post-Run #7). Young baby (Léonie, 17 months) — sleep adequate but activity adherence flexed around family constraints. Vitamin D retest due ~July 2026.

plan_7d_ahead.type: Sur site — commute ebike/gravel (~38 km RT)
Sur site — commute ebike/gravel (~38 km RT)

recommendations.text: Execute Run #8 on Sunday only if zero pain 48h+ after Run #7. Target FC ≤137 bpm. If any Achilles/patellar discomfort persists from today's run, replace with 30-min easy bike or walk. Two clean 5 km runs validate the distance plateau before any progression.
recommendations.measurable: Run #8 completed at 5 km, avg HR ≤137 bpm, zero post-run pain >24h

recommendations.text: Monitor Run #7 cardiac cost trend: FC 140 and max HR 161 are elevated vs Run #5 (FC 136, max 153). If Run #8 also shows FC >140 at same pace, investigate accumulated fatigue, dehydration, or heat. Consider adding a recovery week before progressing distance.
recommendations.measurable: Run #8 avg HR ≤137 bpm at similar pace; flag if >140 again

recommendations.text: Rebuild chronic load consistency: decline from 22.3 to 15.6 over 3 weeks reflects irregular activity. Prioritize e-bike commutes on all sur site days and both weekend sessions to stabilize and reverse the chronic load trend.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Avg HR >140 bpm for two consecutive 5 km runs at similar pace
markers_to_watch.reason: Run #7 showed elevated cardiac cost (FC 140, max 161) vs Run #5 (FC 136, max 153). Upward trend could signal fatigue accumulation or insufficient recovery.

markers_to_watch.threshold: Below 15 = extended detraining; target gradual increase to 20+ over next 2 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: Dropped from 22.3 (May 14) to 15.6 (Jun 3). Continued decline widens gap from historical peak (currently 0.97%).

markers_to_watch.threshold: Any localized joint/tendon pain lasting >24h after a run triggers skip of next run
markers_to_watch.reason: At 103 kg with increasing run distance (5+ km), tissue tolerance remains the key constraint per return-to-running protocol.
